summary: "Pause All" and "Manual Override" buttons in the Agent Orchestration header are non-functional — no click handler is wired, so clicking them does nothing (zero network calls, zero UI/state change).
reproduction: (Account is free-plan, so the route normally shows the SubscriptionGate paywall — see MV-agent-monitor-004; the Orchestration section only renders when the entitlement check fails open, or on a paid account)
Once the Orchestration section is visible, click the "Pause All" button in its header
Click the "Manual Override" button next to it
Observe network tab and page state before/after each click
expected: A user-facing control that is prominently labelled and styled as a primary action (especially "Manual Override", styled with an amber accent) should either perform the implied action, open a confirmation/detail flow, or be visibly disabled/removed if not implemented.
observed: Neither button fires any API call or produces any visible state change. Source: apps/web/src/components/agents/Orchestration.tsx:92-106 — both <button> elements have no onClick prop at all.

summary: Task Queue progress percentages for in-progress ("active") runs are a hardcoded formula (35 + i*25 => 35%/60%/85%), not derived from any real backend progress value — AgentRun has no progress-fraction field, so any percentage shown for a running/queued task is fabricated.
reproduction: No field on the AgentRun API model (apps/web/src/lib/api/agents.ts:15-26) carries a progress fraction — status is only queued/running/completed/failed
expected: A progress percentage displayed to the user should reflect a real measured value (or the UI should show an indeterminate/spinner state instead of a specific fabricated number).
observed: Any run in running/queued state is assigned 35%, 60%, or 85% purely based on its list position, regardless of actual progress. NOTE: not observed rendering live in this session because no run was in running/queued state during the test window (all recent runs were completed/failed) — this finding is [INFERRED] from source, not [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] of the live fabricated number; the completed-task branch of the same widget (100% for finished tasks) WAS observed live and is honest.
evidence: test-artifacts/17-failopen-task-queue.png (shows only the honest 100%-completed branch, live)

summary: Two widgets on the same screen report conflicting "success rate" and total task-count figures for what a user would read as the same metric, because they are computed over different data windows with no disclosure.
reproduction: Login, reach /dashboard/agents Orchestration section (see MV-agent-monitor-004 for how)
Read the "Performance" card: shows 50 tasks run / 82.0% success rate
Read the "Success Rate" stat card in the row above: shows "last 160 tasks" / 66.9% success rate
Both are visible in the same full-page screenshot
expected: A single, consistent success-rate figure per screen, or a clear label disclosing that the two figures use different sample windows.
observed: Orchestration "Performance" card computes client-side from `GET /agents/runs` (backend default limit=50) => 50 tasks / 82.0%. The "Success Rate" stat card reads `GET /agents/stats` (backend limit=200, actual returned taskCount=160) => 66.9%. Root cause: apps/web/src/components/agents/Orchestration.tsx:52-54 computes successRate from the `runs` prop (capped at 50 by apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1123-1125 `limit: int = 50`), while apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:2120-2164 `/agents/stats` uses `limit=200`. Neither widget labels its sample window.
evidence: test-artifacts/session2-results.json (reproduced identically in fresh session: 82.0%/50 vs 66.9%/160)

summary: The entire /dashboard/agents monitoring UI (Orchestration graph, task queue, performance, error log, recent runs, agent stats) is replaced by a full-page "Subscribe to unlock Aether" paywall for the canonical manual-verification test account (admin/admin123, free plan) — this is DOCUMENTED, INTENTIONAL product behaviour (GAP-P6-PAYWALL, apps/web/src/components/subscription-gate.tsx), not a code defect, but it means genuine browser-level testing of this screen's interactive elements is impossible on the assigned test account without a workaround. A secondary, related observation: the same SubscriptionGate is coded to FAIL OPEN (render the real dashboard, including live spend/token/run data) if its own `GET /billing/entitlement` call errors — verified live by intercepting and aborting that one request, which is how this tester reached the real Orchestration UI at all. That fail-open path is also explicitly documented/intentional, but is worth product-owner awareness: a transient network hiccup on a single read-only status endpoint causes a free user to see read data (spend, token counts, provider credential hints, full run history) that the subscription paywall is otherwise designed to withhold, even though write/run actions remain separately blocked server-side by the 402 gate on every actionable endpoint.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123, navigate directly to /dashboard/agents — observe the paywall (test-artifacts/01-agents-loaded-fullpage.png, v2-01-direct-load-paywall.png, reproduced twice)

In a fresh session, use Playwright route interception to abort GET /api/billing/entitlement, then navigate to /dashboard/agents — the real Orchestration/monitoring dashboard renders instead of the paywall (test-artifacts/14-failopen-fullpage.png)
expected: Either: (a) orchestrator provides a PAID test account so this and other /dashboard/* screens can be tested normally, and/or (b) product owner confirms the fail-open read-data exposure is an acceptable, intentional trade-off (as the source docstring already argues).
observed: Confirmed both behaviors live, twice, exactly as documented in apps/web/src/components/subscription-gate.tsx:1-18.

summary: Workflow Graph renders as a simple grid/row of 8 cards with one decorative dashed line, not the wireframe's directed node-graph diagram (nodes connected by distinct SVG edges reflecting real data flow). Positive counterpart: production correctly REPLACED the wireframe's 6 aspirational/partly-fictional nodes (Discovery, Evaluator, Tailoring, Submission, Learning, Memory — several of which are 'planned', not real, agents) with the real, accurate 8-agent pipeline (Supervisor, Discovery, Evaluator, Matcher, Tailoring, Cover Letter, Stories, Email), per the component's own comment: "no phantom nodes" (Orchestration.tsx:10-13). This is a deliberate, correct divergence from the wireframe, not a defect — only the pure graph-layout simplification is flagged here.
reproduction: Compare design/screens/agent-monitor.html (SVG node-graph with connecting lines at specific x/y coordinates) against the live rendering
expected: N/A for pixel-parity (protocol does not require pixel-perfection) — noted as a minor visual-fidelity gap only.
observed: Cards laid out in a responsive grid, one shared dashed animated line behind them, no per-edge connectors.
